content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6-max

## Summary

Reviewed amendment fr-20-e5031cbe concerning the non-round-trippable parse/format pair for failure outcome details. The bug is real: format_failure_outcome_detail writes the error_summary verbatim on the 'error=' line, but parse_failure_outcome_detail splits every line on semicolons before matching prefixes. An error message containing ';' will be truncated, and substrings after semicolons that happen to match 'task_id=' or 'attempt=' patterns will corrupt the parsed structured fields. This breaks idempotency of reconcile_failure for replayed runs when the original error message contained semicolons.

## Votes

- `fr-20-e5031cbe`: Accept
  The bug is verifiable by tracing the code: format_failure_outcome_detail (line 584) writes 'error={error_summary}' verbatim, but parse_failure_outcome_detail (line 593) applies .split(';') across all lines before matching prefixes. If error_summary contains a semicolon, the error= value is truncated at the first ';', and any trailing fragments that happen to start with 'task_id=' or 'attempt=' will override the real structured fields. This is not theoretical — error messages from external tools routinely contain semicolons (e.g. shell errors, multi-clause messages). On replay, the mutated outcome_detail differs from what was originally stored, which means reconcile_failure writes a new value instead of detecting the existing one as unchanged, violating the idempotency guarantee that existing tests assert. The remediation hint is sound: either parse only the fixed header lines by position (task_id= on line 1, attempt= on line 2, error= as the remainder from line 3 onward) or switch to an unambiguous encoding like JSON.
